message:
Support from_nic, read_pcap, and write_pcap (#6022)

<sub>resolves TNZ-82, TNZ-499, TNZ-108, TNZ-109</sub>

## 🔍 Problem

- Live NIC capture and PCAP handling were inconsistent across
packet-capture operators and tests.
- PCAP coverage still lived partly under `test-legacy/`.

## 🛠️ Solution

- Add `from_nic` as an event source with `read_pcap` as the default
parser.
- Add BPF capture filters to `from_nic`.
- Support `read_pcap` and `write_pcap` throughout the packet-capture
workflow.
- Move PCAP coverage into `test/tests/operators/pcap/` and add focused
operator tests.
- Add a changelog entry and companion docs update.

## 💬 Review

- Please focus on the streaming logic in `ReadPcap`, the header handling
in `write_pcap`, and the `from_nic` capture path.
- I also manually validated `from_nic "en1"` locally.

## Documentation PR

- https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/259

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+---
+title: Raw-byte hashing for binary values
+type: bugfix
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - codex
+pr: 6022
+created: 2026-04-16T07:29:26.558216Z
+---
+
+The `hash_*` functions now hash `blob` values by their raw bytes. This makes checksums computed from binary data match external tools such as `md5sum` and `sha256sum`.
+
+For example:
+
+```tql
+from_file "trace.pcap" {
+  read_all binary=true
+}
+md5 = data.hash_md5()
+```
+
+This is useful for verifying file contents and round-tripping binary formats without leaving TQL.

@@ -1,5 +0,0 @@
-
-// Make sure basic decapsulation logic works.
-from f"{env("TENZIR_INPUTS")}/pcap/example.pcap.gz"
-this = this.decapsulate()
-write_tql compact=true

@@ -1,5 +0,0 @@
-
-// Decapsulate an SLL2 frame.
-from f"{env("TENZIR_INPUTS")}/pcap/sll2.pcap"
-this = this.decapsulate()
-write_tql compact=true

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+error: `filter` must not be empty
+ --> tests/operators/from_nic/error_empty_filter.tql:5:23
+  |
+5 | from_nic "lo", filter=""
+  |                       ^^ 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+error: pipeline must return events
+ --> tests/operators/from_nic/error_parser_returns_bytes.tql:5:15
+  |
+5 | from_nic "lo" { pass }
+  |               ^ 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+// Make sure basic decapsulation logic works.
+from_file f"{env("TENZIR_INPUTS")}/pcap/example.pcap.gz" {
+  decompress_gzip
+  read_pcap
+}
+this = decapsulate(this)
+write_tql compact=true

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+// Re-produce an identical copy of the input by taking the input PCAP file
+// header as blueprint for the output trace. The MD5 of the original input
+// is bead1d02a0c44484e51b00b00c3fc869.
+from_file f"{env("TENZIR_INPUTS")}/pcap/big-endian-single-packet.pcap" {
+  read_pcap emit_file_headers=true
+}
+write_pcap
+read_all binary=true
+hash = data.hash_md5()
+drop data
